title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The CDC confirmed the new COVID-19 variant BA.3.2 in Massachusetts, first detected in South Africa in 2024.
BA.3.2 evades antibodies from 2025-2026 vaccines aimed at JN.1 strains, potentially reducing vaccine effectiveness.
WHO reports BA.3.2 poses low additional public health risk and hasn't increased hospitalizations or deaths.

title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A lawsuit against Massachusetts DCF workers Katheryn Butterfield and Candice Gemski for child removal in Waltham will proceed.
The lawsuit alleges unconstitutional removal of Sarah Perkins and Josh Sabey's children, despite no evidence of abuse.
Federal judge ruled sufficient facts exist to question whether parents consented to children’s removal by DCF workers.

title: 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Three New England restaurants are among Food & Wine's 12 most anticipated openings for spring 2026.
Willie's in Beacon Hill, from chef Jamie Bissonnette, offers pizzas and global-influenced pastas.
Ladyfish in Portland, Maine, and La Tavernetta in East Boston are slated to open by May 2026.

title: The Boston Public Library is the star of Kate Quinn’s latest NYT bestseller
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Kate Quinn's latest novel, 'The Astral Library,' debuts at No. 2 on the New York Times bestsellers list.
The novel, set in Boston, features the Boston Public Library as a magical portal to a fantastical library.
Quinn, a Boston University alum, wrote her debut novel in the Boston Public Library's Bates Hall Reading Room.

title: Councilors, Pressley doubling down on demands for bodycam footage after deadly police shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston City Council calls for updated policies for releasing police bodycam footage after Roxbury shooting.
The resolution, led by Councilors Culpepper and Worrell, seeks timely access for oversight officials to footage from major events.
Rep. Ayanna Pressley supports the call, emphasizing transparency and accountability for the families involved in the March 11 incident.

title: New England’s only dual-launch straddle coaster to open at Six Flags
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch straddle coaster, opens April 11 at Six Flags New England in Agawam.
The new coaster accelerates riders to 45 mph and features 11 airtime moments and a stengel dive.
Six Flags New England, with over 100 attractions, opens to season pass holders April 11 and to the public April 17.

title: A new program will make Boston the 1st major-city school district to require AI training
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston will launch AI training for all high school students, starting September, in a first for a major U.S. city.
The program, supported by a $1 million grant from Kayak co-founder Paul English, includes teacher training by UMass Boston.
The curriculum focuses on ethical AI use, preparing students for technological, academic, and leadership roles in the future workforce.

title: After mix-up, Matthew Farwell’s lawyers amend court filing, say unnamed person fathered Sandra Birchmore’s unborn child
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matthew Farwell's lawyers amended a court filing, clarifying that an unnamed person, not his brother, fathered Sandra Birchmore's child.
Farwell is a former Stoughton police officer accused of killing Sandra Birchmore in 2021; his trial is expected in October.
Farwell's lawyers blamed The Boston Globe for a misreading, causing misinformation about the child's paternity in the revised footnote.

title: A Boston student runs the Marathon to honor her best friend — and the father she lost
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lily Penzone, 21, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her friend's father who died in a trauma accident.
Penzone is participating on behalf of Brigham and Women's Stepping Strong team to support trauma research and recovery.
Abby's father, honored through this run, was a multi-time Boston Marathoner known for his generosity and care.

title: Boston College senior running Boston Marathon to support first responders
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Merideth Greene, a Boston College senior, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Last Call Foundation.
Greene was inspired by the bravery of first responders during the 2013 Boston Marathon bombing and its aftermath.
The Last Call Foundation aims to improve firefighter safety through cancer screenings, safer gear, and health advocacy.
